title: Harden GitHub Actions CI security
description:
The skeptic review identified security gaps in the GitHub Actions workflow (.github/workflows/ci.yml):
- actions/checkout@v4 uses a mutable tag; should be pinned to a SHA for supply-chain integrity
- The workflow lacks a permissions block, running with default write access
- No workflow_dispatch trigger for manual runs

Update the CI workflow to address these issues.

title: Add README troubleshooting section
description: Add a Troubleshooting section to the README covering common issues: (1) htop shows only container processes instead of host processes, (2) permission denied errors, (3) terminal display issues. Include the correct docker run flags for each scenario. This improves user experience and reduces support burden.

title: Add OCI metadata labels to Dockerfile
description: Add standard OCI annotation labels to the Dockerfile including org.opencontainers.image.title, org.opencontainers.image.description, org.opencontainers.image.source, org.opencontainers.image.version, and org.opencontainers.image.licenses. These labels improve discoverability and compliance with container best practices.

title: Update Alpine base image to 3.21
description: Update the Dockerfile FROM directive from alpine:3.19 to alpine:3.21 to incorporate the latest security patches and package updates. Ensure htop remains available and functional in the new base image. Run make test after the change to verify.

title: Add functional smoke test and fix .gitignore
description:
The current test (`docker run ... which htop | grep '/usr/bin/htop'`) only verifies the binary exists.

Add a functional smoke test:
- Verify htop binary is executable (`htop --version`)
- Also fix .gitignore to exclude editor swap files (*.swp, *.swo) to prevent accidental commits like the .AGENTS.md.swp incident.

title: Replace Travis CI with GitHub Actions
description:
The project currently uses Travis CI (.travis.yml) which is largely deprecated/unreliable for open source.

Migrate to GitHub Actions with a workflow that:
- Builds the Docker image
- Runs the test target
- Supports multi-arch builds if feasible

Remove .travis.yml as part of this work.

title: Modernize Dockerfile for best practices
description:
The current Dockerfile uses deprecated practices:
- MAINTAINER is deprecated, should use LABEL
- FROM alpine without a tag
- Separate RUN apk update and RUN apk add htop (creates extra layer)
- Missing --no-cache for apk

Update the Dockerfile to follow modern Docker best practices.

title: Update README with modern usage instructions
description:
The README references Travis CI and outdated Docker commands. Update it to reflect:
- GitHub Actions build badge instead of Travis
- Modern docker run syntax
- Build instructions using make
- Any other improvements for clarity

Depends on the GitHub Actions migration bead.

title: Add .dockerignore and improve build hygiene
description: Add a .dockerignore file to prevent unnecessary files from being included in the Docker build context.\n\nAlso review and improve the Makefile (add .PHONY targets, ensure clean builds, etc.).
